complete_examples.overview: Complete workflow examples for debugging DarkDelve issues using MCP validation.

example.name: mcp_bug_validation_and_test_creation
example.scenario: A bug is reported where the player cannot move when adjacent to a wall. The LLM playtester may not reliably reproduce this issue.
example.user_request: Debug: Player cannot move when adjacent to a wall. Need to validate with MCP and create a test.

step.approach: Initialize a game, position player near a wall, attempt movement, verify bug.
Write a test that reproduces the bug and will catch regressions.
step.analysis: Test should position player near wall, attempt movement, verify correct behavior.

step.approach: Read error messages, check dungeon_map logic, verify coordinate system.
Fix the specific logic error in movement code.
Run the new test and all existing tests.

example.completion: Bug validated using MCP, test created, root cause identified, minimal fix applied, all tests pass.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Tests should be created before fixing to ensure regression coverage
Minimal fixes are easier to verify and less likely to introduce new bugs

example.name: combat_damage_calculation_bug
example.scenario: Combat damage calculation is producing incorrect results.
example.user_request: Debug: Combat damage is not calculating correctly. Validate with MCP and create test.

step.approach: Create test entities with known stats, execute combat, verify damage calculation.
Write test_combat_damage_calculation_bug() that verifies expected damage formula.
Check CombatResolver.resolve_attack() implementation, verify damage formula.
Fix the specific line in damage calculation.
Run test_combat_damage_calculation_bug() and all tests.

example.completion: Combat damage bug validated, test created, root cause identified, fix applied, all tests pass.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Combat tests should use deterministic entity stats
Damage formula should be verified against game design
Always test edge cases (critical hits, minimum damage, etc.)

example.name: inventory_pickup_bug
example.scenario: Player cannot pick up items from the ground.
example.user_request: Debug: Player cannot pick up items. Validate with MCP and create test.

step.approach: Position player on item, execute pickup action, verify item is added to inventory.
Write test_inventory_pickup_bug() that verifies pickup mechanics.
Check pickup_item() implementation, verify inventory weight limits, check item spawning.
Fix the specific logic error in pickup or weight calculation.
Run test_inventory_pickup_bug() and all tests.

example.completion: Inventory pickup bug validated, test created, root cause identified, fix applied, all tests pass.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Inventory tests should verify both success and failure cases
Weight limits should be tested with edge cases
Always verify item is removed from map after pickup

example.name: monster_spawning_in_walls
example.scenario: Monsters are spawning inside walls instead of on floor tiles.
example.user_request: Debug: Monsters spawning in walls. Validate with MCP and create test.

step.approach: Generate a level, check all monster positions against dungeon_map.
Write test_monster_spawning_in_walls() that verifies all monsters are on floor tiles.
Check spawn logic in generate_level(), verify dungeon_map coordinate logic.
Fix the spawn condition to check for floor tiles (False) not walls (True).
Run test_monster_spawning_in_walls() and all tests.

example.completion: Monster spawning bug validated, test created, root cause identified, fix applied, all tests pass.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Always verify dungeon_map[x, y] logic: True=wall, False=floor
Spawn tests should check all entity positions
Include both monster and item spawning in tests

example.name: fov_coordinate_swap_bug
example.scenario: Field of view is centered on wrong position when player is off-diagonal.
example.user_request: Debug: FOV centered on wrong position. Validate with MCP and create test.

step.approach: Position player at non-diagonal coordinates, compute FOV, verify center position.
step.expected_outcome: FOV is centered on wrong coordinates

step.approach: Write test_fov_coordinate_system() that verifies FOV center matches player position.
Check FOVSystem.compute() implementation, verify pov parameter order.
Fix the coordinate order in compute_fov() call: pov=(safe_x, safe_y).
Run test_fov_coordinate_system() and all tests.

example.completion: FOV coordinate bug validated, test created, root cause identified, fix applied, all tests pass.

key_takeaways.takeaway: DarkDelve uses dungeon_map[x, y] indexing, not [y, x]
FOV pov parameter should be (x, y) not (y, x)
Always test FOV with off-diagonal player positions
